mission.title: cloud-custodian: Access Key Groupings
mission.description: Access Key Groupings. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current cloud-custodian deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cloud-custodian version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cloud-custodian -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloud-custodian
helm list -n cloud-custodian 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cloud-custodian installation.

steps.title: Review cloud-custodian configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cloud-custodian configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cloud-custodian -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloud-custodian
kubectl get configmap -n cloud-custodian -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cloud-custodian
```
If an IAM account has never been used the "access_keys.last_used_date" will be absent.  I want to disable access keys that have never been used AND the last rotated key is greater than 60 days.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Access Key Groupings
steps.description:
parse is not needed as k['CreateDate'] is already a datetime object.

Adjusting the policy in https://github.com/capitalone/cloud-custodian/issues/842 and setting the `action` phase to the following solves this issue.

```
    actions:
      - type: remove-keys
        disable: true
        age:
```yaml
name: iam_accounts-disable-never_used
    description: |
       Disable IAM accounts that have never been used in the last 60 days
    resource: iam-user
    filters:
      - type: credential
        key: access_keys.active
        value: true
      - type: credential
        key: access_keys.last_used_date
        value: absent
      - type: credential
        key: access_keys.last_rotated
        value_type: age
        value: 60
        op: gte
    actions:
      - type: remove-keys
        age: 60
        disable: true
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cloud-custodian -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloud-custodian
kubectl get events -n cloud-custodian --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Access Key Groupings" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
parse is not needed as k['CreateDate'] is already a datetime object.

Adjusting the policy in https://github.com/capitalone/cloud-custodian/issues/842 and setting the `action` phase to the following solves this issue.

```
    actions:
      - type: remove-keys
        disable: true
        age: 90
```
codeSnippets:
name: iam_accounts-disable-never_used
    description: |
       Disable IAM accounts that have never been used in the last 60 days
    resource: iam-user
    filters:
      - type: credential
        key: access_keys.active
        value: true
      - type: credential
        key: access_keys.last_used_date
        value: absent
      - type: credential
        key: access_keys.last_rotated
        value_type: age
        value: 60
        op: gte
    actions:
      - type: remove-keys
        age: 60
        disable: true

      "arn": "arn:aws:iam::xxxxxxx:user/read-s3",
      "password_last_used": null,
      "password_last_change

actions:
      - type: remove-keys
        disable: true
        age: 90

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cloud-custodian installed or the issue environment reproducible.
